title: inspect/copy does not work with tarballs with "./" prefix
body:
Doing anything that involves a docker-archive which has a "./" for all contents fails. (e.g. the path to the manifest in the tarball is `./manifest.json` instead of simply `manifest.json`)
OTOH, `docker load` is able to read these tarballs, so my best guess is that skopeo should try to support this as well.

I've already proposed a 'fix' for this issue upstream: https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1000, but since you've vendored the dependency, you've also vendored the issue :confetti_ball: :stuck_out_tongue: 

If the upstream fix is not accepted, would you consider patching your vendored copy to work in this situation instead? Thanks in advance.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out and for opening the PR!

> If the upstream fix is not accepted, would you consider patching your vendored copy to work in this situation instead? Thanks in advance.

The maintainers of c/image and skopeo (along with most github.com/contianers/* projects) are effectively the same set of people :) Once your issue is fixed, we can cut a patch release and update the go modules here.
This seems to be fixed in the recent 1.2.0 release
